title: silene squamigera subsp. squamigera
intro: Silene squamigera subsp. squamigera, also known as the woolly catchfly, is a perennial plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its woolly stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Silene squamigera subsp. squamigera has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting oxidative stress, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate respiratory issues and support overall health.
